**Detecting Bias: A Time-Travel Investigation**

Ten years in the future, the player will suffer a serious injustice : his apprentice/friend/neighbor/… (“apprentice” if we consider the player as a private detective), \<name\>, will be wrongly judged by an AI risk assessment system and nothing can be changed… except if you could go back in time. His worst nightmare takes shape before his eyes, he is going to lose one of the persons he cherishes the most (like his son/daughter). Just when everything seems lost (we can imagine a death penalty), the player discovers a time machine.

The player travels back to the past to investigate how the AI system became biased before it is officially deployed.

To uncover the truth, the player takes on the role of a detective (or if he is originally a private detective, we can more say that “he uses his sharp skills of detective”), following clues through the AI pipeline: first the data, then the algorithm, and finally the humans who interact with and shape the AI system.

**Main Character**  
Player Role: **The Detective**

The player is a detective from the future (“who”, instead we would have “whose apprentice/friend/neighbor/…”) was wrongly convicted by an AI justice system. Their mission is to travel back in time, investigate where bias entered the AI pipeline, and prevent the same injustice from happening again.

The player gradually discovers that AI bias does not come from only one source. It can appear in the training data, in the algorithm’s definition of fairness, and in the way humans guide, judge, or rely on AI systems.

**note : side kick \= apprentice/friend/neighbor (I was lazy to write everytime)**

We are in \<year\>, \<city name\>, at the golden age of technology. The humans succeeded to create an artificial intelligence that is more fair than any human and most importantly way faster. They deployed it for many tasks where fairness in decision-making is important, like loan application, criminal judgment and … (other examples). Our player lives a peaceful life in a pretty neighborhood. They only have a dog and \<apprentice/neighbor/friend/…\> as company, but they are satisfied with that. Everything seems perfect, until one day. A clear sky, the sun was irradiating and no one was on the streets. The door bell rang. Weird, because the player was not expecting someone. The \<side-kick character\> was watching some tv show in the living room. When they opened the door they got blinded by the sudden intense luminosity. With difficulty, the player was able to discern two silhouettes of policemen. They told them that they were here to take \<side-kick name\>, that he/she is charged with a felony. Without any additional explanations, they intrude themselves in the house and handcuff the poor \<side-kick\>.

Later, the player learns that \<side-kick\> is charged with espionage of governmental offices. But when he talked to \<side-kick\>, he/she told them that someone just gave him a few bucks in exchange for putting some \<object\> in the pocket of a man (business man outfit). And because he/she was from a group with a high recidivism rate, the AI decided to punish him/her more strictly (what punishment ?). He/she was framed or at least was part of a more evil plan against his/her will. And because the AI was not able to see this detail, now \<side-kick\> is sentenced to \<penalty\>. 

In despair, the player tried everything to change the decision, but because everything is governed/controlled by AI for justice (to avoid bribery or manipulation), it didn’t work. 

A few days have passed, the morale is very low, the ambiance is gloomy around the player (drinking, no self care at all). He is slowly becoming part of the furniture, watching tv all day. Suddenly the door bell rings. When he opened the door, a mysterious small guy was standing. He told him his empathy about the dramatic situation and offered his help. From what he says, he built a time machine. And he set the date to some weeks before the deployment of the AI model. One thing remains to do … it is to correct this AI and save \<side-kick\>

In **Chapter 1**, the player investigates the AI’s training data and discovers **sampling bias**. The model was trained on too few people from only part of the city, so it performed well on limited examples but failed when applied to the whole population. The player helps collect better data by choosing which regions to investigate, how many people to include, and what information to collect.

After the data is improved, the model becomes more accurate. However, the player soon learns that better data is not enough.

In **Chapter 2**, the player investigates **algorithmic fairness bias**. The AI still has to choose what “fair” means. The player first experiences their own human bias by quickly judging defendant profiles, then chooses a fairness definition for the AI. From the judge’s perspective, the system seems fair because it is consistent, efficient, and statistically justified. But from the defendant’s perspective, the same system may still feel unfair because it can reproduce historical inequality, use proxy variables, and ignore individual context. Even after trying different fairness definitions, the problem persists. The detective decides to look at the data and discover that the real issue is not the algorithm, but the data itself (data bias). And it is not due to the sampling method, but in reality it is the humans that create this bias in data (cycle in crime).

In **Chapter 3**, the player investigates **human-in-the-loop bias** in LLMs. The player discovers that even when an AI system is improved, human choices can still shape the AI’s thinking. The player receives a set of question cards and must decide how to answer or label them. These human choices are then used to guide or fine-tune an LLM. As the player continues, they see that their answers influence how the AI responds later. If the player gives biased, incomplete, or one-sided feedback, the AI begins to reflect those patterns in its own outputs.

By the end of the story, the player understands that AI bias is not caused by a single mistake. It is created through a chain of decisions: what data is collected, what fairness rule is chosen, and how humans guide or correct the AI.

![][image1]

**Scene 1**

In the future city of Novus, humanity has handed its hardest choices to AI.  
It decides who receives a loan, who gets a job, who enters school, and who is found guilty.  
People believe it never gets tired, never takes sides, and is never influenced by emotion.  
In this city, AI is known as **“the fairest judge.”**

## **Scene 2**

In one quiet corner of the city, a private detective lives a peaceful life.  
By his side are only a loyal dog and a young apprentice.  
They do not have a luxurious office or world-shaking cases.  
But in an age ruled by machines, this small home still holds a little warmth.

## **Scene 3**

Until one ordinary morning, the doorbell rings.  
The sound is short and cold, as if fate itself is knocking.  
Police officers are standing outside.  
They offer little explanation and say only one thing:  
the apprentice has been accused of helping steal confidential government documents.

## **Scene 4**

The apprentice says he knows nothing.  
A stranger simply gave him a little money and asked him to slip a small device into a businessman’s pocket.  
He thought it was just a strange errand, a harmless little task.  
But he did not know he had already been pulled into a much larger conspiracy.

## **Scene 5**

The AI does not see his panic.  
It does not see that he was used, nor the truth hidden behind the incident.  
It sees only data: his background, his neighborhood, and people from similar past cases.  
Then the system delivers a cold conclusion:  
**High risk.**

## **Scene 6**

In court, the detective desperately tries to explain.  
He presents evidence, tells the full story, and tries to make people see what really happened.  
But before the judge, a human voice seems too slow, too fragile.  
The AI’s judgment is clean, fast, and filled with certainty.  
At last, the gavel falls.  
Not because the truth has been seen, but because the machine has already made its decision.

## **Scene 7**

The days that follow become long and silent.  
The case files pile higher on the desk, and the clues on the wall grow more tangled.  
Every page points to doubt. Every clue proves that some truth is still unseen.  
But the city has already moved on.  
Only the detective remains trapped in that day, unable to move forward.

## **Scene 8**

Just as he is about to give up, a stranger appears at the door.  
He carries a machine that should not exist, and a hope that sounds almost impossible.  
“I cannot change the verdict now,” he says.  
“But I can send you back to before it all happened.”  
Back to before the AI was deployed.  
Back to before bias became a verdict.  
Back to the moment when the future can still be changed.
